name: DEAP — Database for Emotion Analysis using Physiological Signals
description: A multimodal affective-computing dataset in which 32 participants watched 40 one-minute music videos while 32-channel EEG and peripheral physiology were recorded, rating each video on valence, arousal, dominance, and liking. Widely used as a benchmark for decoding emotional states from brain and body signals.
citation: Koelstra S, Muhl C, Soleymani M, et al. (2012). DEAP: A Database for Emotion Analysis using Physiological Signals. IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing, 3(1), 18-31.
